siteFooter.tagline: A seeded voxel world protocol on Solana.

header.title: World Generation Algorithm
header.body: NiceChunk terrain is generated from a public seed pipeline. The same seed and coordinates produce the same terrain profile, biome, water, caves, trees, and block query results.

seed.title: Seeded world state
seed.body: A text seed is hashed once, then mixed into every coordinate sample.

height.title: Heightfield first
height.body: Continents, oceans, relief, rivers, dunes, and mountains produce the base terrain.

biome.title: Climate and biome pass
biome.body: Temperature, humidity, slope, altitude, water, and special masks select the biome.

block.title: Block query rules
block.body: The engine answers block requests from depth, fluid, cave, surface, and vegetation rules.

hashSeed.title: Hash seed
hashSeed.body: Convert the visible seed into a stable integer used by all random functions.

sampleFields.title: Sample fields
sampleFields.body: Read continentalness, erosion, weirdness, temperature, humidity, volcanic, and corruption noise.

buildHeight.title: Build height
buildHeight.body: Blend ocean basins, land shelves, lowland relief, rivers, dunes, mountains, and slope limits.

chooseBiome.title: Choose biome
chooseBiome.body: Apply the ordered biome priority so water, cold, altitude, wetness, heat, and forest masks resolve consistently.

answerBlocks.title: Answer blocks
answerBlocks.body: Return terrain, fluid, caves, cave walls, vegetation, trees, and decorations from coordinate queries.

biomes.label: Biome priority
biomes.title: Water and extreme terrain win first

deterministicSeed.title: Deterministic Seed
deterministicSeed.description: The seed is reduced to a stable integer and mixed into every random sample. Terrain height, climate, water, trees, caves, and material rolls are deterministic for the same seed and coordinates.

noiseToolkit.title: Noise Toolkit
noiseToolkit.description: The generator is built from simple seeded noise primitives. FBM creates broad variation, ridge noise creates bands and channels, and smoothstep turns noisy values into soft biome and feature masks.

columnProfile.title: Column Profile
columnProfile.description: Every world column is summarized into a terrain profile. Rendering, collision, minimap color, trees, and water all read from this same profile, so the page is explaining the actual runtime data path.

continentOceanMask.title: Continent And Ocean Mask
continentOceanMask.description: Very low frequency noise decides broad ocean basins, shelves, coastlines, and inland land before local hills are added. Shelf and inland masks blend sea floor into raised land instead of switching abruptly.

baseLandHeight.title: Base Land Height
baseLandHeight.description: Current client constants are chunkSize 16, seaLevel 96, and landBaseHeight 100. Oceans sit below sea level, coasts rise through the shelf, and inland columns start near the land base before hills and dunes are applied.

lowlandRelief.title: Lowland Relief
lowlandRelief.description: Flat land is not perfectly flat. Multiple medium-frequency noise layers create plains, low hills, dry washes, and small ridges while keeping the terrain readable at walking scale.

desertDunes.title: Desert Dunes
desertDunes.description: Deserts come from hot, dry, broad climate noise. When a desert mask is strong enough, extra dune height is added on inland land using ridge and FBM noise.

riverCanyonLakeCarving.title: River, Canyon, And Lake Carving
riverCanyonLakeCarving.description: Rivers, canyons, and lakes are carved into the base height field before normalization. River paths use warped line noise, canyons cut deeper in dry terrain, and lake basins flatten low inland areas.

mountainCells.title: Mountain Cells
mountainCells.description: Mountains are seeded per 192-column cell. A cell may have no mountain, and each visible column checks nearby cells so large mountain footprints can cross cell boundaries.

mountainShape.title: Mountain Shape
mountainShape.description: A mountain is an angled, warped ellipse. Long and short radii vary independently, then local coordinate warping bends the ridge so mountains do not look like perfect cones.

mountainHeightTexture.title: Mountain Height And Texture
mountainHeightTexture.description: Large mountains are rare but possible. Final elevation combines falloff shoulders, a central spine, ridge crags, erosion, and summit roughness, then takes the maximum over all candidate mountains.

slopeLimiter.title: Slope Limiter
slopeLimiter.description: After raw height is computed, a local pass limits cliffs against nearby columns. Lowlands stay gentle, while mountain areas are allowed to be rougher without becoming unbounded spikes.

heightNormalization.title: Height Normalization
heightNormalization.description: The final height preserves underwater basins, keeps carved low land above sea level when it is no longer ocean, and keeps regular land from dropping below the land base.

climateSampling.title: Climate Sampling
climateSampling.description: Temperature and moisture are broad fields. They create large hot, cold, wet, and dry regions that later decide desert, tundra, rainforest, forest, swamp, and snow.

biomePriority.title: Biome Priority
biomePriority.description: Biome selection is ordered. Water and special terrain win first, then altitude and slope, then wet/cold/hot climate categories, with plains as the fallback.

waterProfile.title: Water Profile
waterProfile.description: Water is part of the terrain profile, not a post-process. Oceans fill to sea level; rivers, lakes, swamps, and wetlands can place water above local ground; visual depth is capped for readable rendering.

caveCells.title: Cave Cells
caveCells.description: Caves are generated on demand per block query. They only appear below the near-surface layer, get easier to form when deeper, and expose walls when adjacent cells are open.

chunkRenderingStrategy.title: Chunk Rendering Strategy
chunkRenderingStrategy.description: The rule preview page uses the same terrain generator but loads chunks progressively. Near chunks keep full decoration; farther chunks use lighter detail so fast world browsing stays responsive.

depthLayers.title: Depth Layers
depthLayers.description: Block type depends on vertical depth first. Bedrock anchors the bottom, deep stone fills low layers, ordinary stone fills deeper terrain, and biome-specific layers appear near the top.

waterEdgeSurfaces.title: Ocean, Beach, River, And Lake Surfaces
waterEdgeSurfaces.description: Water-edge biomes use sediment blocks. Oceans and beaches can show shell beds, rivers bias toward gravel and clay, and lakes use clay or sand with some gravel variation.

volcanoSurface.title: Volcano Surface
volcanoSurface.description: Volcano biomes appear only when volcanic noise is very high and above sea level. Their surfaces are mostly basalt and ash, with rare lava cells near the hottest vents.

coldMountainSurfaces.title: Mountain, Snowfield, And Tundra Surfaces
coldMountainSurfaces.description: Cold and high terrain exposes rock, snow, frozen soil, ice, and gravel. Gentler mountain shelves can still carry grass, which keeps mountain bases playable.

desertSurface.title: Desert Surface
desertSurface.description: Deserts are mostly sand-like terrain but can contain quicksand, salt flats, and dry dirt. Weirdness noise creates extra quicksand patches independent of the normal surface roll.

swampWetlandSurfaces.title: Swamp And Wetland Surfaces
swampWetlandSurfaces.description: Wet areas blend solid ground with shallow water-like surfaces. Corrupted swamp samples can become toxic water; wetlands stay gentler with clay, mud, and grass.

landSurfaces.title: Rainforest, Forest, And Plains Surfaces
landSurfaces.description: Regular land surfaces are climate-sensitive. Rainforest is wetter and muddier, forest can grow moss in humid cells, and plains shift slightly toward dry dirt or clay at climate extremes.

subsurfaceRules.title: Subsurface Rules
subsurfaceRules.description: The block below the top surface also follows biome. This makes cliffs and mined side faces match the local environment instead of turning every column into the same dirt layer.

naturalFluids.title: Natural Fluids
naturalFluids.description: Fluid blocks are selected after biome. Cold underwater cells become ice, corrupted wet areas may become toxic water, swampy areas use swamp water, and volcano vents can emit lava.

fluidHeight.title: Fluid Height
fluidHeight.description: Different fluids use different visible levels. This lets rivers and lakes sit above local floors while oceans stay at a shared sea level.

waterDepthRendering.title: Water Depth Rendering
waterDepthRendering.description: Visual water depth is capped at four blocks. The cap keeps underwater columns cheap to render and prevents deep basins from overwhelming the preview.

aquaticDecoration.title: Aquatic Decoration
aquaticDecoration.description: Plants can also come from water cells. Coral is restricted to shallow warm oceans, dead coral marks cold or corrupted water, and reeds appear rarely near non-ocean wet biomes.

dryHotVegetation.title: Dry And Hot Vegetation
dryHotVegetation.description: Desert decoration only appears on appropriate dry surfaces. Cactus requires an additional coarse roll so it appears as scattered stands instead of uniform noise.

wetForestVegetation.title: Wet And Forest Vegetation
wetForestVegetation.description: Wet regions favor ground cover, vines, roots, reeds, and mushrooms. Rainforest gets the strongest boost, while regular forest stays mostly grass and bushes.

coldMountainVegetation.title: Cold And Mountain Vegetation
coldMountainVegetation.description: Cold biomes use sparse hardy decoration. Steep slopes suppress most vegetation except mountain-specific decoration, which keeps cliffs visually clean.

treeDensity.title: Tree Density
treeDensity.description: Trees are controlled by biome, slope, altitude, forest strength, meadow noise, and biome boosts. Rainforest and forest receive stronger boosts, while high altitude reduces density.

treeTypeLeaves.title: Tree Type And Leaves
treeTypeLeaves.description: Tree shape and color are deterministic per area. Cold and high locations prefer pines, rainforest can use giant roots and teal/dark leaves, and normal forests vary leaf tone by seed roll.

caveWallBlocks.title: Cave Wall Blocks
caveWallBlocks.description: A solid cell beside an open cave becomes an exposed cave wall. Deep caves use deep stone and glow mycelium more often; volcanic or corrupted caves can expose dangerous fluids.

caveDecoration.title: Cave Decoration
caveDecoration.description: Cave surfaces can carry small decorations. Deep caves favor glow mycelium, humid caves can produce mushrooms or moss, and lichen appears as general cave cover.

generatedBlockQuery.title: Generated Block Query
generatedBlockQuery.description: The engine does not pre-store full terrain. It answers block queries from the profile and depth rules, which lets rendering, collision, mining, and water simulation read the same procedural world.

findNearestCave.label: Search
findNearestCave.notFound: No cave was found near the current coordinates.
